title: Pasta alla Norma
subtitle: Sicilian aubergine, tomato, basil, ricotta salata.
excerpt: Cubes of aubergine fried in olive oil until silky, tossed with a quick tomato sauce, basil, and a final blanket of ricotta salata, all bound through rigatoni. Sicily's mid-century pasta classic. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Sicilian dish from Catania, named for Bellini's opera Norma. The technique here matches the consensus served in Catanese trattorie. Pre-1928 public-domain Italian cookbooks do not document the dish under this name.

content.text: Cubed aubergine fried in plenty of olive oil until the cubes are tender and crisp at the edges. A quick passata sauce simmered with garlic and basil. Rigatoni boiled until al dente, then tossed through the sauce with the aubergine and finished with a snowfall of ricotta salata. Forty minutes, one of the great vegetarian pastas.

Ricotta salata is hard, white, salted ricotta; pecorino works at a pinch but the texture is different. The aubergine wants real oil, a tablespoon won't get them silky. Cube them small, salt them briefly, fry them properly.

items.name: Aubergine
items.prepNote: around 350 g each, cubed into 2 cm pieces

items.name: Tomato passata
items.prepNote: or a tin of plum tomatoes blended

items.name: Fine sea salt
items.prepNote: for the pasta water and salting the aubergine

content.text: Tip {{aubergine}} of cubed aubergine into a colander. Scatter generously with salt. Toss. Leave for 15 minutes; the salt draws out water and bitterness. Pat dry on kitchen paper.

Heat {{olive-oil}} of olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat until it shimmers. Fry the aubergine cubes in two batches, turning often, for 8 to 10 minutes a batch until deep gold and tender. Lift onto kitchen paper to drain. Reserve a tablespoon of the oil.

Set the sauté pan over medium heat with the reserved tablespoon of aubergine oil. Add {{garlic}} of sliced garlic; cook 60 seconds until fragrant but not coloured. Pour in {{tomato-passata}} of passata. Season with salt and pepper. Tear in half the {{basil}} of basil. Simmer 15 minutes until thickened and glossy.

Bring 4 L of generously salted water to a boil. Drop in {{pasta-dried}} of pasta. Cook for 1 minute less than the al dente time on the packet. Lift the pasta into the sauce with a slotted spoon along with a ladleful of pasta water.

Add the fried aubergine to the pan. Toss for 60 seconds over medium heat; the pasta finishes cooking and absorbs the sauce. Stir through the rest of the basil and a drizzle of {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of extra-virgin olive oil.
Twist the pasta onto four warm plates. Snow a generous grating of {{pecorino-romano}} of ricotta salata or pecorino over each. Eat hot.

items.fix: Heat the oil until it shimmers before adding aubergine. Fry in batches so the pan stays hot.
items.cause: Pan too cold when the aubergine went in; cubes absorbed all the oil.

items.fix: Salt for 15 minutes in a colander; pat dry. Modern hybrids are less bitter but the salt still firms the cube.
Simmer for 15 minutes uncovered until the sauce coats a spoon.
Lift pasta from the water into the hot sauce; never rinse.
items.cause: Drained and rinsed, sauce added cold.

content.text: Norma was named for Bellini's 1831 opera; the dish came later and the Catanesi call something exquisite 'una vera Norma'. Sicilian aubergines, Sicilian tomatoes, Sicilian ricotta salata, three island ingredients in one bowl. A glass of dry Sicilian white.
